text: ‘And your suit shall prosper’; literally, ‘and you shall go’. The verb seems to be used as in Jg. 4.24; the rendering ‘and you shall go and plead with me and I will listen’ breaks the run of the sentence.
Literally, ‘because you say that’. Some think the order of the text has become dislocated, and this verse ought to come between verses 20 and 21.
